Well, it was in the middle of summer in July, and it was a full moon in 2011.
I had worked all day in the woods, ironically, picking wood for a bed that I was going to make.
And so I was really in tune with the sounds of the forest and stuff like that from being in the woods all day.
I returned home at about 4.30, 5 o'clock, and my wife and her friend were getting ready for a trip that we had planned to go on to one of her friend's weddings, which was down at Pagosa Springs.
It's gorgeous down there. Lots of really tall pine trees, lots of really fragrant underbrush there like sage and lots of berries at this time of year.
So you'd expect to see bears and things like that walking around on the road because it's just easier.
And it's such a remote area where Wolf Creek Pass is located that you can imagine seeing quite a few different animals.
I've seen moose there before.
I've seen all kinds of things, mountain lions.
I've seen a pack of wolves down there.
And they're not supposed to be there, ironically.
enough but I've seen there overlooking the valley off of the same overlook I always stop there
it's one of my favorite places but we loaded up and were driving in my wife's friend's minivan
and I was driving doing all the driving duties and the ladies were doing all the
party prep duties. I've never really been a drinker. I've never even had a single cup of
coffee in my whole life or smoked a single cigarette. So I'm always the designated driver.
So I was driving and we drove all the way through Colorado, no incident.
And everyone fell asleep. My two-year-old son was in the car in his car seat at the time.
ironically his name's river danger and uh they were all sleeping and they were sleeping pretty
heavily i guess by the time i got to wolf creek pass and came over the continental divide i saw
how gorgeous the full moon was it was such a nice night you could smell the pine in the air
and it was just an idyllic drive for me.
It was so quiet, which is a rare thing for me being a father.
And just the ideal drive that you could possibly think of.
And when I got to the Wolf Creek Valley pull-off,
I decided to pull off the road and I had to just take in the view
and stretch my legs a little bit, take care of things.
And I don't know, I was overlooking the valley with the full moon.
It was almost like 11, 1130 at night, and the moon was pretty high up in the sky,
so it was lighting up almost like day the whole valley.
And you could see all the Wolf Creek running down,
feeding all the plants and everything along the side,
and you can see all the forest off to the side away from the water source.
And there's a lot of open fields there.
There's a lot of game there.
There's pristine mountain water.
And it's a really ideal place for wildlife to live.
So I guess as a place to live, that would be an excellent place
if you were something like the Dogman.

My hackles went straight up.
Like my spidey sense started tingling.
And for some reason, I just decided, nope, I need to get out of here.
It was the most uneasy feeling I can ever remember having in my entire life.

I mean, you could see almost as bright as day.

and I don't know if it's just because it stands out in my memory because of the event that

I decided just to drop it into drive, and I pulled out, and as I turned on the lights,
probably about 100 yards down the road, I saw a large black figure running on all fours.
And my very first thought was, oh, wow, is that a black bear?
running along the side of the road in plain sight.
And I was getting ready to wake up my son so he could see it.
But luckily I did not.
I kept my eyes on it and I kept going.
And I kept seeing this glint of red as it was probably looking back behind it as it was running.
There was really nowhere for it to go.
It could have gone across the front of me and gone off the cliff, which at that point was pretty sheer.

And then on the other side, there was a pretty sheer cliff that it would have been probably not very difficult for it to climb, but a whole lot less difficult than just running down the side of the road.

But as I picked up speed a little bit and got a little closer, because I really had no fear of driving past a black bear, but as I got closer, I noticed that the tail was much longer than a bear tail.
and it looked more like a canine tail and at that point I also noticed that the hind legs had a

That a lot of people have lost with the advent of instant access to information.
